figures.caption: Figure 1: Compute–accuracy frontier. Each point is one (condition, benchmark) pair. TrACE-4 and TrACE-8 (red, pink) match the accuracy of SC-4 and SC-8 (blue) at substantially fewer LLM calls, Pareto-dominating the self-consistency baseline in both settings. Error bars on MiniHouse are 95% bootstrap CIs; GSM8K bars are Wald CIs from binomial data (no per-task data available for bootstrap, see §6).
Figure 2: Left: Agreement is a reliable success signal. Right: TrACE call counts are task-adaptive.

Figure 3: τhigh ablation (TrACE-4, kmax = 4). Three thresholds are evaluated: 0.50 (commit at bare majority), 0.75 (default), and 1.00 (require unanimity, degenerates to SC-4). The dotted line shows SC-4 accuracy and call count for reference. TrACE with τ = 0.75 achieves matched accuracy at substantially fewer calls than either extreme. τ = 0.50 underspends and slightly loses accuracy; τ = 1.00 recovers SC-4 accuracy and cost.
